prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, gather 'round ye scallywags and lend me yer ears, for Cap'n will tell ye the mystery of that great blue expanse above our heads!

Ye see, when the mighty sun - that blazin' golden doubloon in the heavens - sends its light down upon us, that light be carryin' all manner of colors within it, like treasure hidden inside a chest. Red, yellow, green, and blue, all bundled together like a crew below decks.

Now, our sky be filled with tiny... particles, invisible to the naked eye, like the smallest grains of sea salt ye ever did see. And when that sunlight crashes into these tiny devils, the blue light - bein' the shortest and quickest of the bunch, like a nimble cabin boy - gets scattered and thrown about in every direction across the whole of the sky. The other colors, the reds and the yellows, they be longer and slower, like a fat merchant vessel, and they pass straight on through without much fuss.

So when ye look up from the deck of yer ship, no matter which direction yer weathered eyes be turnin', that scattered blue light be comin' at ye from everywhere at once!

And THAT, ye mangy sea dogs, be why the sky above be as blue as the very ocean we sail upon! Now back to yer posts before I have ye swabbin' the poop deck till Davy Jones takes ye!
